title: PitchBook Data FOIA Request: University of Kentucky Endowment

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am currently seeking updated quarterly performance data from your plan’s alternative asset holdings for the following date(s): 1Q16,3Q20,1Q25,2Q25,3Q25,4Q25 & 1Q26.
As always, thank you in advance for taking the time to send the requested documents. Below is the formal request.
On behalf of PitchBook Data, Inc., under Kentucky Open Records Act, I request a copy of the quarterly public records from 1Q16,3Q20,1Q25,2Q25,3Q25,4Q25 & 1Q26 for the following information, preferably in Excel or PDF format.
1.	Names and vintage years of all private equity, venture capital, mezzanine, distressed, real estate/REIT, debt and infrastructure partnerships in University of Kentucky Endowment's portfolio.
2.	Commitments made to each partnership.
3.	Contributions drawn down since inception.
4.	Distributions made to University of Kentucky Endowment to date by each individual partnership.
5.	Net Asset Value of each partnership.
6.	Internal rates of return (IRRs) for each partnership with and without the use of credit facility. Please note if the IRRs are not net.
7.	Investment multiple (TV/PI) for each individual partnership.
8.	The dollar amount of “total management fees and costs paid” for each individual partnership.
9.	Date as of which all the above data was calculated.
10.	Names of all alternative asset partnerships partially and fully sold by University of Kentucky Endowment, including date of sale.
11.	For each fund, please indicate whether or not the fund uses Subscription Credit Facilities.
I also request a copy of any disclosable documents which contain some or all of the following information:
12.	Names of all hedge fund holdings (including hedge fund of funds) in University of Kentucky Endowment's portfolio.
13.	Month by Month Market Value for each hedge fund holding (including hedge fund of funds) since University of Kentucky Endowment's initial investment up to 1Q26.
14.	Month by Month Contributions to each hedge fund holding (including hedge fund of funds) since University of Kentucky Endowment's initial investment up to 1Q26.
15.	Month by Month Returns (net of fees) earned by University of Kentucky Endowment for each hedge fund holding (including hedge fund of funds) since initial investment up to 1Q26.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kelly Lyles

title: OPM/DOGE "What Did You Do Last Week?" Employee Accomplishment Reporting Records (February–March 2025)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Freedom of Information Act Request

Drug Enforcement Administration
Freedom of Information/Records Management Section

Re: OPM/DOGE "What Did You Do Last Week?" Employee Accomplishment Reporting Records (February–March 2025)

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request the following records:

1. Copies of any email, memorandum, directive, instruction, or communication received by DEA employees between February 21, 2025 and March 7, 2025 relating to requests that federal employees report their weekly accomplishments, including but not limited to emails bearing the subject lines:

   • "What did you do last week?"
   • "What did you do last week? Part II"

2. Copies of any responses submitted by DEA employees between February 22, 2025 and March 7, 2025 in response to the above-described accomplishment-reporting requests.

3. Copies of any DEA-wide guidance, legal review, management directive, talking points, Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs), policy memoranda, or internal communications created, sent, or received between February 21, 2025 and March 14, 2025 concerning whether DEA personnel should respond to the requests.

4. Any records reflecting:
   a. The number of DEA employees who received the requests;
   b. The number who responded;
   c. The number who failed to respond;
   d. Any tracking, compliance, disciplinary, or follow-up measures associated with the requests.

5. Any records concerning the collection, review, storage, analysis, dissemination, retention, or use of employee responses submitted between February 22, 2025 and March 14, 2025.

6. Any communications between DEA personnel and the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), the Executive Office of the President, DOGE personnel, the Department of Justice, or any other federal agency regarding implementation, compliance, legal review, or handling of the accomplishment-reporting requests between February 21, 2025 and March 14, 2025.

To the extent that responsive records contain personal identifying information, I request that such information be redacted and that all reasonably segregable portions be released.

The requested records concern the operation and management of the federal government and will contribute significantly to public understanding of government activities. I therefore request a waiver of all fees. In the alternative, please notify me if fees are expected to exceed $25.

The records should be provided electronically.

Requested Action if Records Are Withheld:
• Conduct a de novo review of withheld materials;
• Release all non-exempt and segregable portions of the records;
• Provide a detailed justification or Vaughn index explaining the application of each exemption;
• Reconsider whether any redacted content can be released in light of the public interest.

Respectfully,

William Fernandes

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes

title: Chinese Entering the United States Claiming Citizenship

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Chinese Entering the United States Claiming Citizenship
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records captioned "Chinese Entering the United States Claiming Citizenship", including file 105-HQ-12752. This program was described in a 1975 memo as "Identification of Chinese legally entering the U.S. with intelligence assignments". (https://www.archives.gov/files/research/jfk/releases/2018/docid-32989624.pdf).

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. This is also to include any responsive records held in offsite locations, such as the Alexandria Records Center. Please include both investigatory and non-investigatory records in your search. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

title: Mandatory Declassification Review Request – NMCC and JCS Recall Orders (USS Liberty)
tags: israeli attack on the uss liberty

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Mandatory Declassification Review Request – NMCC and JCS Recall Orders (USS Liberty)
communications.communication:
To the Declassification Officer:

This is a request for a Mandatory Declassification Review (MDR) under Section 3.5 of Executive Order 13526.

Description of Records:
I am requesting a formal declassification review of all records, logs, and message traffic originating from the National Military Command Center (NMCC) and the Joint Chiefs of Staff (JCS) between 1400Z and 1600Z on June 8, 1967, regarding the recall of carrier-based aircraft launched to defend the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

This request specifically includes:

    The recorded logs and transmission text of the "Flash" messages sent to Vice Admiral William I. Martin (COMSIXTHFLT) and/or Rear Admiral Lawrence R. Geis (Cardiv 20) ordering the return of aircraft to the USS Saratoga and USS America.

    Any recorded voice transcripts or handwritten notes from the NMCC Duty Officer regarding orders issued by Secretary of Defense Robert McNamara or the White House concerning these specific flight launches.

Justification:
These records are nearly 60 years old. The operational methods of the NMCC in 1967 are obsolete and well-documented. Continued classification serves no current national security interest and prevents a full historical accounting of the command decisions made during the attack.

Joseph L. Meadors
USS Liberty Survivor
Board Member and Historian
USS Liberty Veterans Association (https://ussliberty.org)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Chat histories of all ChatGPT sessions conducted by City Councilmembers and the Mayor on city-owned devices or used in job-related functions:

The timeframe for this request is 01/01/2026 to the date this request is processed.

For reference, please share with respondents the following help article and instructions for exporting ChatGPT histories into a .zip folder:

https://help.openai.com/en/articles/7260999-how-do-i-export-my-chatgpt-history-and-data

1. Sign in to ChatGPT.
2. In the top right corner of the page click on your profile icon.
3. Click Settings.
4. Click Data Controls menu.
5. Under Export Data click Export.
6. In the confirmation screen click Confirm export.
7. You should receive an email with your data. Note: The link in the email expires after 24 hours
8. Click Download data export to download a .zip file will be downloaded including your chat history in chat.html as well as your other data on file with us

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Benjamin Anthony Morea (Bookchin's friend)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into anarchist artist Benjamin Anthony Morea (October 8, 1941 – May 2, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/26/us/ben-morea-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Robert Leon Woodson Sr. (April 8, 1937 – May 19, 2026)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into conservative activist Robert Leon Woodson Sr. (April 8, 1937 – May 19, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/05/20/us/politics/robert-woodson-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Please provide a copy of the current contract between the Paulding County and Flock Safety, including the terms, duration, and financial details of the agreement.

2. What specific data is being collected by Flock's cameras, and how long is this data retained? Please include details on any facial recognition or license plate recognition technologies employed.

3. Who within the department has access to the data collected by Flock, and with whom is this data shared? Please specify any agreements or protocols for data sharing with external agencies or entities.

4. What measures are in place to protect the privacy of individuals captured on Flock's cameras? Please provide any privacy impact assessments or policies related to the use of this technology.

5. Please provide any incident reports or internal investigations related to the misuse or malfunction of Flock's technology, including any instances of false positives or wrongful accusations.

6. What steps has the department taken to inform and engage the community about the use of Flock's technology? Please include any public meetings, notices, or feedback mechanisms implemented.

7. Can you provide data on the effectiveness of Flock's technology in reducing crime or enhancing public safety? Please include any studies or reports that evaluate the impact of this technology on our community.

8. Please provide access logs detailing who has accessed the data collected by Flock's cameras, the frequency of access, and the purpose of each access. Include any protocols for monitoring and auditing access to this data.

9. Are there any plans to renew or extend the current contract with Flock Safety? If so, please provide details on the proposed terms, duration, and any changes from the existing agreement.

10. Has the department considered or evaluated alternative vendors or technologies for similar purposes? Please provide any comparative analyses or evaluations conducted.

I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to your prompt response. Please send the requested information to the address provided above or via email at ShonnyG112@proton.me.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Gershon Chapman

title: ICE Access and Immigration-Enforcement Coordination Records — Industry Station / Hacienda Heights (TRUTH Act)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: ICE Access and Immigration-Enforcement Coordination Records — Industry Station / Hacienda Heights (TRUTH Act)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request concerns records that the Transparent Review of Unjust Transfers and Holds Act (the TRUTH Act, Assembly Bill 2792, Government Code sections 7283 and following) expressly designates as public records subject to the California Public Records Act, namely all records relating to access provided by the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department to United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement, including all communications with that agency concerning such access. The request also concerns the Department's compliance with the California Values Act (Senate Bill 54, Government Code sections 7284 and following). I am directing this request to records associated with the Industry Station and its patrol responsibility for the unincorporated community of Hacienda Heights, and I am requesting records covering the period from January 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed.

I request, first, all records of ICE access to any individual in the Department's custody, as the term access is used in the TRUTH Act, including access logs, the written consent forms provided to individuals before any interview, and any records documenting whether such access was granted or declined. Second, I request copies of all immigration detainers, notification requests, transfer requests, administrative warrants, and Forms I-247, I-200, and I-205 received from Immigration and Customs Enforcement or United States Customs and Border Protection, together with the Department's response to each. Third, I request all communications, including emails, letters, memoranda, text messages, and call or visit logs, between Department personnel and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Customs and Border Protection, or the Department of Homeland Security concerning access to, notification about, or the transfer of any individual.

Fourth, I request all policies, directives, training bulletins, unit orders, and any memoranda of understanding or written agreements governing the Department's interaction with federal immigration authorities, including any provisions that address the use of private contractors to carry out transfers of individuals to federal custody. Fifth, I request all records reflecting Department personnel participation in, or support of, any federal immigration enforcement operation conducted within the Industry Station service area, including records of perimeter security, traffic control, scene assistance, or any officer-safety assist function provided in connection with such an operation. Sixth, I request all data, reports, and presentation materials that the Department prepared for or provided to the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors in connection with any community forum held under the TRUTH Act, along with records identifying the dates on which such forums were held.

I understand that personal identifying information may be redacted before disclosure, and I am not seeking that information; a permissible redaction does not justify withholding an otherwise responsive record. If any responsive record is withheld in whole or in part, please identify the record and state the specific exemption claimed, and please note that the TRUTH Act independently designates records relating to ICE access as public. I would appreciate rolling production of records as they are located rather than a single delayed release, and I am willing to narrow or clarify any category upon request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Avila

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Haugan, 70/30 Violations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

In the November 20, 2025 memo written by Conrad Timbers, Acting Chief Portfolio Officer, he states on page 4, "Moreover, their class size compliance review found that ten of forty-two K–8 classrooms are currently out of alignment with the Illinois State Board of Education (ISBE) 70/30 rule. OSD’s report recommends that the master schedule is revisited to ensure efficient use of instructional time and staffing and immediately addressing the issues with the ISBE 70/30 rule."

Provide records sufficient to show the Class Size Compliance Review. 

This could include any reports, notes, dashboard views, lists or other memoranda that records the 70/30 misalignment. I am not seeking any student information so please redact any student specific information. Information in the aggregate is not identifiable. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

communications.subject: FOIA Request :: N019720-052826
communications.communication:
Dear Miriam : 
 
Thank you for your interest in Chicago Public Schools. Your FOIA request has been submitted. Your FOIA Center reference number for tracking purposes is: N019720-052826 
You have requested the following records: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

In the November 20, 2025 memo written by Conrad Timbers, Acting Chief Portfolio Officer, he states on page 4, "Moreover, their class size compliance review found that ten of forty-two K–8 classrooms are currently out of alignment with the Illinois State Board of Education (ISBE) 70/30 rule. OSD’s report recommends that the master schedule is revisited to ensure efficient use of instructional time and staffing and immediately addressing the issues with the ISBE 70/30 rule." 

Provide records sufficient to show the Class Size Compliance Review. 

This could include any reports, notes, dashboard views, lists or other memoranda that records the 70/30 misalignment. I am not seeking any student information so please redact any student specific information. Information in the aggregate is not identifiable. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Attachments: 

https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/miriamcb/177876/SB630_Colocation_Memo_to_CEO_King__11_20.pdf 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2302624/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

Documentation sufficient to explain the City of Detroit’s residential valuation methodology for the 2024 and 2025 assessment rolls, including:

- Economic Condition Factor (ECF) studies and calculations
- Residential land value tables
- Depreciation schedules
- Cost tables used in residential valuation
- CAMA valuation methodology documentation
- Assessment model specifications or calibration materials
- Internal guidance or manuals used by appraisal staff

communications.subject: FW: [EXTERNAL] Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Detroit tax formula
communications.communication:
This email serves as the City of Detroit’s response to the above-referenced matter. Your request was received at the City of Detroit Law Department Freedom of Information Act Section via facsimile or email on May 28, 2026. Because your request was received by electronic transmission, pursuant to Section 5(1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act (the “Act”), MCL 15.235(1); it is deemed to have been received at the Law Department on the next business day, May 29, 2026.

Pursuant to Section 5(2) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2), the City’s response is due within five (5) business days. However, due to the nature and the scope of your request and the volume of the requests received by the City, we are extending the City’s response deadline by ten (10) additional business days in accordance with Section 5(2) (d) of the Act, MCL 15.235(2) (d). Therefore, your request will be granted, denied, or granted in part and denied in part on or before June 22, 2026.

Your request is being handled by Amanda Kelly. If you have questions regarding your request, or if you did not provide an email address in your request, please forward it to Amanda Kelly at kellya@detroitmi.gov<mailto:kellya@detroitmi.gov> to provide you a response more readily than by regular mail or fax. Mail and fax are not preferred at this time since they both require in-office support. We thank you in advance for your understanding.
